---
title: "Continuous Assignment 1"
author: 
  - "Fhatuwani Mokwenda"
  - "Jadene Jafthar"
format:
  pdf: 
    mainfont: "Times New Roman"

engine: knitr
---

## Question 1
```{r}
#| echo: false
#| message: false
#| warning: false

# for libraries
library(ggplot2)
```

```{r}
#| echo: false
#| message: false
#| warning: false
#Split data: training (80%) and testing(20%)
set.seed(123)
trainindex = sample(nrow(listings), size = 0.8*nrow(listings))
train = listings[trainindex, ]
test = listings[-trainindex, ]
```

Exploring the *Price* variable, we see that it is heavily left-skewed. Because of this, we choose a high percentile value in order for the cutoff to represent the most "balanced" variation.
```{r}
#| echo: false
#| message: false
#| warning: false

# picking a cuttoff x
Price <- train$price
cutoff <- 0.75

x <- quantile(Price, cutoff)
hist(Price, 
     main = "Distribution of Price",
     col = "lightblue",
     breaks = 50)
abline(v = x, col = "red")
text()

ggplot(Price) +
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 5, fill = "lightblue", color = "white") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = x, color = "red", linetype = "dashed", size = 1) +
  annotate("text", x = x, y = 8, label = paste("Cuttof =", index_value),
           color = "red", hjust = -0.1) +
  labs(title = "Histogram with Index Line", x = "Value", y = "Count")



```


\newpage

## Question 2
\newpage

## Question 3
\newpage

## Question 4
